# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build_and_test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20' # Or your application's required Node.js version

      - name: Cache Node.js modules
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-node-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build

      - name: Upload production build artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: production-build
          path: dist # Adjust this path to your application's built artifact directory (e.g., build/, public/)

  deploy:
    needs: build_and_test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: Production
      url: https://your-app-production-url.com # Replace with your application's URL
    steps:
      - name: Download production build artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: production-build
          path: .

      - name: Configure AWS credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: us-east-1 # Replace with your AWS region

      - name: Deploy to S3 and Invalidate CloudFront
        run: |
          # Sync built files to S3 bucket. --delete ensures old files are removed.
          # Adjust the --exclude patterns based on what should NOT be deployed from the artifact.
          aws s3 sync . s3://your-production-bucket-name/ --delete \
            --exclude "package.json" --exclude "package-lock.json" \
            --exclude "node_modules/*" --exclude "src/*" --exclude ".git/*" \
            --cache-control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" # Example cache control for static assets

          # Invalidate CloudFront cache to serve new content immediately
          aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id YOUR_CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID --paths "/*"
